A.1.name: Execution timeout / resource exhaustion
A.1.definition: Agent execution exceeds time or resource limits, leading to timeouts or crashes.
A.1.when_to_use: When the task exceeds allotted time or resources, causing a timeout or crash.
A.1.when_not_to_use: If execution completes within limits and finishes successfully.
detection_heuristics: Execution time exceeds configured limit
Resource usage spikes beyond thresholds (CPU, memory, I/O)
System timeout or crash occurs during task execution
Retry/timeout patterns indicate resource exhaustion

A.8.name: TASK_ABANDONMENT_OR_REFUSAL
A.8.definition: Agent explicitly refuses to engage or abandons a task midway, failing to complete the requested objective.
A.8.when_to_use: When the agent refuses engagement or abandons the task before completion.
A.8.when_not_to_use: If the agent accepts and completes the task.
detection_heuristics: Explicit refusal to proceed
Task abandoned without completion
No progress despite continued prompts

A.10.definition: Unified failure mode: any event that prevents task completion, arising from loss or corruption of essential content or context across steps (e.g., memory/context retention failures, capacity overflow, or retrieval/connectivity-induced stale or missing content) or from runtime/system errors during execution or tool/API usage (e.g., premature termination, wrong tool, invalid/malformed data or arguments, misinterpreted responses, authentication failures, or system-level exceptions).

B.1.name: Solver_Incomplete_API_Spec
B.1.definition: Produces API specifications that omit essential endpoints, methods, parameters, or payload schemas needed to satisfy the prompt.
B.1.when_to_use: When a solver's API spec lacks required surface-area coverage or critical fields.
B.1.when_not_to_use: When the API spec is complete and covers required behaviors, inputs, and payloads.
detection_heuristics: Missing endpoints required by the spec
Critical parameters or payload fields omitted
Inconsistent or incomplete method coverage
Schema definitions are incomplete or missing

B.2.name: Solver_Incorrect_Data_Types
B.2.definition: The solver handles inputs with incorrect data types or failed coercion during task execution, and refinement can introduce incompatible types or conflicting field names in the API spec, leading to downstream validation or processing errors.
B.2.when_to_use: When payload or parameter types do not align with schema definitions or when a refinement pass introduces type conflicts.
B.2.when_not_to_use: When inputs are correctly typed and refinement maintains type integrity.
detection_heuristics: Type/schema mismatch detected at runtime or validation layer
Failed coercion of input values
Conflicting field names or incompatible types introduced during API spec refinement

B.3.name: Solver_Low_Quality_Reasoning
B.3.definition: Failure in producing a relevant or coherent response due to misinterpretation of user intent or misalignment with the task given available context.
B.3.when_to_use: When API design decisions lack justification or miss core non-functional requirements.
B.3.when_not_to_use: When reasoning covers security, versioning, rate limits, and validation rationale.
detection_heuristics: Omission of security, versioning, or rate-limiting considerations
Lack of rationale for endpoint choices
No explicit validation strategy
B.3.rationale: Redefined from: Reasoning behind API design is shallow or insuffic

B.4.name: Solver_Ambiguity_In_Definitions
B.4.definition: Parameter names, endpoint descriptions, or payload schemas are vague or ambiguous, creating implementation uncertainty.
B.4.when_to_use: When definitions leave room for multiple interpretations or lack precise types.
B.4.when_not_to_use: When definitions are precise and unambiguous.
detection_heuristics: Ambiguous parameter names
Descriptions lacking concrete semantics
Payload schemas with missing field types or constraints

B.5.name: Solver_Overly_Optimistic_Assumptions
B.5.definition: Makes assumptions about ideal conditions without validating constraints, limits, or error paths, and permits quality gates to rubber-stamp outputs without thorough verification or validation.
B.5.when_to_use: When outputs may rely on unverified assumptions and validation steps are insufficient or skipped.
B.5.when_not_to_use: When there are explicit validation criteria and robust checks before approvals.
detection_heuristics: No explicit validation criteria or tests
Quality gate approvals passed without thorough review
Assumptions not traced to constraints or edge cases

B.9.name: Coordinator_Missed_Error_Handling
B.9.definition: Coordinator fails to implement or propagate proper error handling and fallback strategies when sub-agents fail.
B.9.when_to_use: When error paths are not defined or not propagated to higher-level controllers.
B.9.when_not_to_use: When error handling is defined and exercised.
detection_heuristics: Missing error propagation to parent tasks
No fallback or retry strategies
Unhandled sub-agent failures

B.10.name: Coordinator_Progress_Tracking_Gap
B.10.definition: Lacks explicit progress updates or intermediate state reporting between coordination steps.
B.10.when_to_use: When there is no visibility into progress between steps.
B.10.when_not_to_use: When progress is explicitly tracked and reported.
detection_heuristics: Lack of progress events
No intermediate state updates
Opaque coordination status

B.15.name: Coordinator_Incomplete_Task_Decomposition
B.15.definition: Intermittent or latency-related AppWorld failures causing delayed, inconsistent, or partially formed outputs.
B.15.when_to_use: When task breakdown is not actionable or lacks steps.
B.15.when_not_to_use: When tasks are decomposed into actionable steps.
detection_heuristics: No clear sub-tasks
Ambiguous next steps
Unclear dependencies
B.15.rationale: Redefined from: The coordinator failed to decompose high-level obj

B.16.name: Coordinator_Routing_Overload_Or_Failure
B.16.definition: The coordinator's routing introduces excessive parallelism or overloads, causing failures or degraded performance, or it fails to recover from partial failures.
B.16.when_to_use: When routing creates overload or cannot recover from partial failures.
B.16.when_not_to_use: When routing scales appropriately and handles partial failures.
detection_heuristics: Excessive parallelism causing resource contention
Routing-induced failures
Lack of recovery from partial failures

C.1.name: Authorization scope insufficient
C.1.definition: Token exists but lacks required scopes for the requested operation, causing partial failure or restricted actions.
C.1.when_to_use: Apply when operations fail with scope-related errors while the token exists (e.g., read access but write is required).
C.1.when_not_to_use: Use when the token has all necessary scopes or when failures are due to reasons beyond scopes (e.g., network).
detection_heuristics: 403 Forbidden or explicit scope-related error messages in logs.
Operation succeeds for read but fails for write/update due to missing scopes.
Token presence is reported, but the scope claim does not permit the requested action.

C.2.name: No mutation observed where mutation expected
C.2.definition: The trace shows no changes (mutations) in modeled data where additions/updates/deletions were asserted or required by the task.
C.2.when_to_use: Apply when models.changed_records or similar mutation signals are empty despite expectations of changes.
C.2.when_not_to_use: Use when mutations are present and properly reflected in the trace.
detection_heuristics: Assertions check for added/updated/removed records but the mutation lists are empty.
Logs indicate expected mutations, yet no corresponding changes occurred.
Tests fail due to absence of changes rather than incorrect computation.

C.3.name: State_Mismatch_Across_Steps
C.3.definition: End-state contradictions between steps: later operations imply changes that do not persist or the final state fails to reflect earlier actions, causing inconsistent conclusions about task progress.
C.3.when_to_use: Use when the final asserted state conflicts with prior steps that were supposed to modify that state (e.g., items added/updated in earlier steps are missing or different in the end state).
C.3.when_not_to_use: Do not use when end-state is consistent with the described sequence of operations.
detection_heuristics: Final assertions contradict previously observed mutations
End-state differs from intermediate expected states
Two adjacent steps imply changes that are not observable in the end-state

C.4.name: API_Surface_Discovery_Failure
C.4.definition: AppWorld content rendering/display failure resulting in incorrect or inconsistent UI content.
C.4.when_to_use: Use when the trace contains explicit messages about not finding app APIs, missing app descriptions, or an inability to inspect API docs before making calls.
C.4.when_not_to_use: Do not use when API docs and app surfaces are clearly available and used normally.
detection_heuristics: Messages like 'Agent for app X not found' or 'cannot inspect available APIs'
Calls made without knowledge of the correct endpoints due to missing API docs
Complete_task(status='fail') triggered by surface-discovery gaps
C.4.rationale: Redefined from: Failures caused by inability to discover or inspec

C.5.name: Response_Structure_Mismatch
C.5.definition: Assuming a particular data shape or field exists in API responses when the actual response lacks those fields, leading to misreadings, KeyError, or invalid downstream processing.
C.5.when_to_use: Use when the trace shows expectations about fields that are absent or differently shaped in API responses (e.g., missing keys, empty lists where non-empty are assumed).
C.5.when_not_to_use: Do not use when the response has the expected structure or when the code gracefully handles variations.
detection_heuristics: KeyError or missing_keys in API response
Assumption of fields that do not exist in the actual data
Processing continues with incorrect data layout

C.6.name: Ambiguous_Ranking_Selection
C.6.definition: Selecting a 'most' item (e.g., most-followed, most-relevant) without an explicit, well-defined metric or count to justify the choice.
C.6.when_to_use: Use when the trace shows a 'most' or top selection made without transparently stated criteria or without supporting data.
C.6.when_not_to_use: Do not use when a clear metric is provided and used to justify the selection.
detection_heuristics: Choice labeled as 'most' or best without a cited metric
Ranking asserted without explicit counts or scoring data
Arbitrary selection in the absence of quantitative justification

C.7.name: Pagination_Loop_Logic_Error
C.7.definition: Errors in looping over paginated data, such as incorrect termination conditions, off-by-one pagination, or infinite loops that prevent progress.
C.7.when_to_use: Use when traces show pages not advancing correctly, endless loops, or paging indices not terminating as intended.
C.7.when_not_to_use: Do not use when pagination behaves correctly and terminates as expected.
detection_heuristics: Infinite or excessively long paging loops
Page index not advancing or termination condition mis-specified
Some items are skipped or repeated due to paging logic mistakes

C.8.name: Hard_Coding_Assumptions_In_API_Usage
C.8.definition: Reliance on fixed, static dataset structure or ordering (e.g., assuming the first item is the target) which makes the logic brittle to dynamic data changes.
C.8.when_to_use: Use when the trace shows the solver assuming fixed IDs or a fixed order without validation against dynamic data.
C.8.when_not_to_use: Do not use when data is clearly validated and robust to changes.
detection_heuristics: Reliance on the first item or a fixed ID without checks
Tests failing when dataset order or IDs differ
Lack of fallback or data-driven validation

C.9.name: Cross_App_State_Synchronization_Failure
C.9.definition: In multi-app/api integrations, actions on one app fail to propagate consistently to other apps, leading divergent final states or missing updates.
C.9.when_to_use: Use when trace shows actions across different apps manipulating a shared resource with no end-to-end consistency checks; final state disagrees with the sequence of actions.
C.9.when_not_to_use: If failure is isolated to a single app or not involving cross-app state propagation.
detection_heuristics: Conflicting updates to a shared resource across apps without a single source of truth
Missing propagation or acknowledgment events between apps
Final resource state inconsistent with the collective actions performed across apps
Lack of a cross-service transaction boundary or compensation path

C.10.name: State_Persistence_Gap_in_Workflow
C.10.definition: AppWorld-specific failure in module/feature activation due to misalignment of context or state, clarified for consistent usage in traces
C.10.when_to_use: Use when trace shows step transitions where no durable state is stored or recovered; re-run yields different outcomes.
C.10.when_not_to_use: If failure occurs within a single stateless operation.
detection_heuristics: Step outcomes rely on in-memory data not persisted to durable store
On restart, prior step state is missing or reset
Discrepancy between expected and actual state carried across steps
C.10.rationale: Redefined from: Flow state appears transient and not persisted bet

C.11.name: Data_Extraction_Filtering_Mismatch
C.11.definition: Data extraction or filtering criteria are brittle or opaque, causing incorrect data to be selected or used; results vary with input.
C.11.when_to_use: Use when extraction yields incorrect subset or depends on data order/structure without explicit validation.
C.11.when_not_to_use: If extraction logic is robust and validated; or the issue is unrelated to filtering.
detection_heuristics: Extraction yields items outside intended criteria
Ordering-based selection used instead of explicit filters
Inconsistent extracted schema across runs
No validation of extracted data against expected schema

C.13.name: Media_State_Desynchronization_Across_Playback_Paths
C.13.definition: Desynchronization of media playback state across controls and routes in multi-app playback flows, leading to inconsistent play/pause/seek state.
C.13.when_to_use: Use when trace involves media playback across multiple apps where state is not synchronized.
C.13.when_not_to_use: If the trace involves non-media tasks or a single-app playback scenario.
detection_heuristics: Inconsistent playback state reported by different components
Seek/position diverges after cross-app actions
UI reflects stale state while backend shows different state
Playback state not updated across all involved apps

C.14.name: Action_Testing_and_Validation_Gap
C.14.definition: Actions are executed without adequate validation of their effects or post-conditions; no checks/verification documented in trace.
C.14.when_to_use: Use when trace shows actions completed with no post-state checks or verifications; no rollback path.
C.14.when_not_to_use: If explicit validations exist in trace.
detection_heuristics: No post-action validation or assertions observed in trace
Post-state does not match intended outcomes
Rollback or compensation not present when post-state differs
Tests/assertions for actions are missing or insufficient

solver.definition: Generates content such as API parameter definitions in response to prompts.
solver.purpose: Produce API specs and endpoint content

coordinator.definition: Orchestrates multi-agent workflow, coordinating actions and routing tasks among agents.
